title.purpose: Panel titles and nav item labels.

body.purpose: Compact interface text and row labels.

label.purpose: Status labels, field labels, and table headers.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The amber command button for refreshing or running a selected tool.
components.css: .ds-button-primary { height: 38px; padding: 0 14px; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; color: #111008; background: #f8bd1c; font: 800 14px/1.45 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; } .ds-button-primary:hover { background: #ffd257; } .ds-button-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid #f8bd1c; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Active Tool Navigation
components.description: The active rail row that shows which tool owns the workspace.
components.css: .ds-nav-active { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; width: 100%; min-height: 54px; padding: 8px 10px; border: 0; border-radius: 8px; color: #f8bd1c; background: #1b180d; text-align: left; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-active span { display: grid; gap: 3px; } .ds-nav-active strong { font-size: 14px; } .ds-nav-active small { color: #b8b29e; font-size: 12px; }

components.name: Tool Library Summary
components.description: A compact rail summary that makes Universal Tools visible before the selected workspace.
components.css: .ds-library-summary { display: grid; gap: 12px; padding: 12px; border: 1px solid #292617; border-radius: 8px; color: #f4f0dd; background: #11120d; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-library-summary span, .ds-library-summary dt { color: #b8b29e; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; } .ds-library-summary strong { font-size: 14px; } .ds-library-summary dl { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr)); gap: 1px; overflow: hidden; margin: 0; border: 1px solid #2a2617; border-radius: 7px; background: #2a2617; } .ds-library-summary div { padding: 9px; background: #0d0e0b; } .ds-library-summary dd { margin: 0; color: #f8bd1c; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 800; }

components.name: Tool Library Workspace
components.description: The inventory workspace for ready and planned Universal Tools utilities. Each ready tool opens its declared workspace route.
components.css: .ds-tool-library { display: grid; gap: 1px; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #292617; border-radius: 8px; background: #292617; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-tool-library article { display: grid; grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) 148px auto; gap: 12px; align-items: center; padding: 13px 14px; color: #f4f0dd; background: #181912; } .ds-tool-library code { color: #f8bd1c; font-family: inherit; font-weight: 800; } .ds-tool-library button { min-height: 34px; padding: 0 12px; border: 1px solid #4a3b13; border-radius: 8px; color: #f8bd1c; background: #151307; font-weight: 800; }

components.name: Release Profile
components.description: A compact Universal Tools identity panel with version, bundle ID, command prefix, and local-data posture.
components.css: .ds-release-profile { display: grid; gap: 12px; color: #f4f0dd; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-release-profile p { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; margin: 0; padding-bottom: 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #292617; } .ds-release-profile span { color: #b8b29e; font-size: 13px; } .ds-release-profile strong { overflow: hidden; max-width: 58%; color: #f4f0dd; font-size: 13px; text-align: right; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Metric Segment
components.description: A flat segmented metric cell for tokens, cost, cache, and coverage.
components.css: .ds-metric { min-height: 112px; padding: 16px; border: 1px solid #302a16; border-radius: 10px; color: #f4f0dd; background: #181912; font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-metric span, .ds-metric small { color: #b8b29e; } .ds-metric strong { display: block; margin: 12px 0 7px; color: #f8bd1c; font-size: 27px; line-height: 1; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Session Table Row
components.description: Dense top-session row with tabular numbers and amber selected state.
components.css: .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; color: #f4f0dd; background: #181912; font: 400 13px/1.45 Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-table td { padding: 11px 10px; border-bottom: 1px solid #292617; text-align: right; } .ds-table td:first-child { text-align: left; } .ds-table .selected td { background: #1e1a0d; } .ds-table .selected td:first-child { color: #f8bd1c; }

narrative.overview: Universal Tools is a local desktop toolbox first. Codex Usage is the first ready tool inside that toolbox, not the product boundary. The app should open like a focused workbench: dark, direct, compact, and ready for repeated use. The default library view does not scan tool-specific private data.
keyCharacteristics: Black workbench shell with amber primary action and active state.
Universal Tools owns the app shell, tool library, and command prefix.
Tool Library is the default entry, not a secondary page.
Tool-specific local data is read only after entering or refreshing that tool.
Codex Usage lives as the first ready workspace inside that shell.
Dense information layout with a wide analysis column and a narrow inspector.
Direct tabular and bar-based data display. No donut charts.
Small radii, crisp dividers, and flat panels instead of decorative shadows.

rules.name: The Tool Shell Rule
rules.body: The rail and breadcrumb must keep Universal Tools visible even when one tool owns the main workspace.

rules.name: The Tool Library Rule
rules.body: The first visible app concept is the Universal Tools library: ready tools, planned slots, and the ut- command prefix.

rules.name: The Default Library Rule
rules.body: Opening the app without a deep link shows Tool Library. Ready tools are entered from there.

rules.name: The Lazy Local Data Rule
rules.body: The default library view does not scan a tool-specific private source. Data access starts only when a tool workspace is opened or refreshed.

rules.name: The Amber Rarity Rule
rules.body: Amber is primary action, active selection, and key state. Do not use it as decoration.

rules.name: The No Donut Rule
rules.body: Usage and model data use bars, rows, and tables. Donut charts are prohibited for this surface.

dos: Do keep Universal Tools visible as the app shell.
Do make the tool library visible before the selected tool's report.
Do open the app at Tool Library unless a specific workspace is deep linked.
Do defer private local scans until the selected tool workspace needs them.
Do make Codex Usage feel like the selected tool, not the whole product.
Do add new ready tools by declaring their command, surface, and workspace route together.
Do expose app identity in Universal Tools terms: version, bundle ID, command prefix, and library counts.
Do use Command Amber for primary action, active state, focus, and selected data only.
Do keep usage displays direct: bars, rows, tables, and clear status text.
Do preserve readable contrast for all small text.
Do keep planned and disabled entries readable instead of fading the entire control.
donts: Don't make Codex Usage look like the only future purpose of the app.
Don't name product-level concepts after the current tool.
Don't use donut charts on this surface.
Don't create privacy-unclear cloud dashboard language or visuals.
Don't expose too many choices before the default path has worked.
Don't add colored side-stripe borders, gradient text, nested cards, or wide decorative shadows.
